message:
perf(core): add prepared hot-cache + IndexedEqualityLookup retained overlay

Two tangled perf features in Connection that land together because
they share the same impl block. Split here only in the commit
message.

1. Single-entry prepared hot-cache (9a409ddb follow-up).
   Repeated identical prepare() calls on the mt-mvcc workload are
   LRU-cache hits, but each hit still pays a hash + RefCell borrow +
   LruCache reorder. Adds three connection-local fields —
   prepared_hot_cache_key: Cell<Option<u64>>,
   prepared_hot_cache_entry: RefCell<Option<Arc<PreparedCacheEntry>>>,
   prepared_hot_cache_needs_lru_touch: Cell<bool> — that short-circuit
   the LRU when the incoming statement hash matches the last one, and
   defer the LRU touch until a schema/function-registry generation
   change actually demands a fresh lookup. The hot cache is cleared
   whenever prepared_cache is cleared, invalidated on schema or
   function-registry generation bumps, and bypassed when
   bypass_compiled_cache is set. All compile/schema invalidation paths
   now funnel through clear_prepared_hot_cache() alongside the
   existing prepared_cache.borrow_mut().clear().

2. IndexedEqualityLookup retained autocommit overlay fast-path.
   PreparedQueryFastPath::SimpleIndexedEqualityLookup was previously
   filtered out by retain_autocommit_overlay_fast_path (fell through
   to None). This wires it into RetainedAutocommitOverlayFastPath as
   a full variant carrying root_page, column_index, parameter_index,
   rowid_alias_column_index, and an Option<Arc<[PreparedMemValueSource]>>
   for the projected form. kind() reports
   "indexed_equality_lookup" vs "projected_indexed_equality_lookup"
   depending on whether projections are present; root_page() returns
   the stored root; the execution path
   retained_autocommit_indexed_equality_rows_in_txn runs the real
   lookup without re-planning.

New bench target crates/fsqlite-core/benches/prepared_cache_hot_paths.rs
exercises two prepare-cycle shapes on the mt-mvcc workload
(INSERT ?1,?2 inside a BEGIN): prepare-hit-only and
prepare→execute round-trip. Cargo.toml registers it as a
harness=false bench so the measurement is stable across cargo bench
invocations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -79,3 +79,7 @@ workspace = true
 [[bench]]
 name = "symbol_ops"
 harness = false
+
+[[bench]]
+name = "prepared_cache_hot_paths"
+harness = false
